steps.title: Use the height formula
steps.content:
For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$, the height is:
$$h=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$$
Substitute the given height:
$$52\sqrt{3}=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$$

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content:
Multiply by $$2$$ and divide by $$\sqrt{3}$$:
$$104\sqrt{3}=s\sqrt{3}$$
$$s=104$$
The answer is B.
